top_signals.title: US stock market to stop shrinking for first time in 23 years
top_signals.summary: Market debuts of SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI plus slowing buybacks could remove vital support for equities, investors warn
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Malaysia Palm Reserves Jump Most in Five Months as Exports Drop
top_signals.summary: Palm oil stockpiles in Malaysia climbed at the fastest pace in five months as a sharp drop in exports overshadowed weaker production in the world’s second-largest grower.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: S. Africa’s Eskom Spinoff Plan Raises Credit Concerns
top_signals.summary: South Africa’s plan to split power utility Eskom Holdings SOC Ltd. into separate units could weaken the state-owned electricity utility’s risk profile because its transmission unit accounts for a major share of its earnings, according to a bondholder and a ratings agency.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Thailand's shrimp industry hit by Malaysia's import ban
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Nippon Steel gets ¥90 billion via first bond since U.S. Steel acquisition
top_signals.summary: The yen-denominated bond sale is an early test of investor appetite for Japanese companies pursuing growth through large overseas acquisitions.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: U.S. Company to Send 250,000 Barrels of Fuel to Cuba
top_signals.summary: An American company is preparing to send a cargo of diesel and gasoline to Cuba in what, if successful, would be the biggest U.S. shipment of fuel to the island nation since the start of the U.S. embargo, Bloomberg reported. Florida-based Vanguard Energy is already exporting gasoline and diesel to Cuba—exclusively working with private buyers. This time, the company is preparing a shipment of 100,000 barrels of gasoline and 150,000 barrels of diesel fuel for the energy-starved island that has been blockaded by the U.S. for several months now…
